name: The Grand Design
tagline: Craterhoof overrun (tutorable; reanimate/Pod/Defense cheat it in); Finale X>=10 backup
idea: Atraxa is a 7-mana 7/7 with flying, vigilance, deathtouch, and lifelink.

briefKills.line: Craterhoof Behemoth + a board (hardcast 8, or Pod / Defense / reanimate / Finale X≥8 cheat it in) → ETB haste: creatures gain TRAMPLE + +X/+X → overrun (reliably decaps; table slower)
Defense of the Heart resolves (any opponent has 3+ creatures at your upkeep) → free: search 2 creatures onto battlefield (Craterhoof straight to play → immediate kill)

openings: Weakest axis — Interaction (3/5): the judges' soft spot.
Ramp-gated; Craterhoof now tutorable (Finale was the single point of failure, fixed 2026-06-23).
Decap never-closes in 5% of goldfish games — a grind risk.
Tables (all three) never-close 62% of the time.

clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T9; the full table around >T12.

gamePlan: Atraxa is a 7-mana 7/7 with flying, vigilance, deathtouch, and lifelink. When she enters the battlefield, reveal the top 10 cards of your library and take one of each card type — typically netting 5–7 cards.
winLine: Craterhoof overrun (tutorable; reanimate/Pod/Defense cheat it in); Finale X>=10 backup

finishers.name: Finale of Devastation at X≥10
finishers.note: Finale searches your library or graveyard for a creature with MV ≤X, puts it onto the battlefield, and if X≥10, ALL your creatures get +X/+X and haste until end of turn.

finishers.name: Defense of the Heart
finishers.note: Sacrifice Defense of the Heart → search your library for two creatures, put them directly onto the battlefield.

finishers.name: Razaketh Reanimation → Tutor Chain
finishers.note: Razaketh’s ability: pay 2 life, sacrifice another creature, search your library for any card.

finishers.name: Chord of Calling → Razaketh
finishers.note: Razaketh enters at end of opponent’s turn.

finishers.name: Protected Kill Turns
finishers.note: Two independent protection methods, both tutorable through the creature engine:

finishers.name: Birthing Pod Chain
finishers.note: Birthing Pod sacrifices a creature to find MV+1, directly to battlefield.

rulings.name: Atraxa starts in the command zone
rulings.note: so Buried Alive / Grisly Salvage cannot mill her. She's only reanimatable after she's died from the battlefield (choose graveyard over command zone). Early reanimation targets are Razaketh, Vilis, Elesh Norn.

rulings.name: Panharmonicon + Elesh Norn = 3× ETBs
rulings.note: (each adds one trigger: base + 1 + 1), not 4×. Elesh Norn also shuts off opponents' ETB triggers.

rulings.name: Restoration Angel can't flicker Karmic Guide
rulings.note: both are Angels, and she only blinks a non-Angel. The other five flicker pieces work fine.

rulings.name: Persist can't reanimate legendaries.
rulings.note: Use it on Karmic Guide, who then returns a legend — a 2-card chain.

rulings.name: Razaketh's sacrifice has no tap cost
rulings.note: activate as many times as you have creatures and life (2 each). This is why Phyrexian Tower does NOT enable the Karmic Guide + Reveillark loop (it taps), but Razaketh does.

rulings.name: Living Death is not a same-turn kill
rulings.note: returned creatures have summoning sickness. Rebuild now, win next turn with Finale's haste.

rulings.name: Teferi, Time Raveler's abilities are loyalty, not ETB
rulings.note: flickering him resets loyalty but triggers nothing.

rulings.name: Reveillark returns power ≤2 only
rulings.note: (Ranger-Captain is a 3/3 — not a valid target). Veil of Summer protects vs. blue/black only.

keep.mixed: ramp-gated; Craterhoof now tutorable (Finale was the single point of failure, fixed 2026-06-23)

mull.strategy: Looking for: mana toward a reanimation or Atraxa line — a dork/rock plus either a graveyard-filler (Buried Alive, Grisly Salvage) and a cheap reanimate, or a Birthing Pod start. Mulligan HARD for acceleration: a dork/rock/land-ramp start is worth +5–6pp on the decap curve (gd_clock_lab keep A/B, 2026-07-03). Four-to-five-land hands full of uncastable bombs with no ramp are the trap keep — ship them every time.

killTree.title: The Grand Design — Atraxa, Grand Unifier
killTree.root:
Atraxa cast — ETB reveals top 10, take one of each card type (nets 5–7 cards)
fuels reanimation + flicker + Birthing Pod creature chain
killTree.stall:
build the engine (reanimate a bomb T3–4, cast Atraxa T6–7, or start a Pod chain) —
the deck grinds at sorcery speed; protect the kill turn behind a counter or Teferi (protection is thin)
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Craterhoof Behemoth + a board
(hardcast 8, or Pod / Defense / reanimate / Finale X≥8 cheat it in)
lines.kill: ETB haste: creatures gain TRAMPLE + +X/+X → overrun (reliably decaps; table slower)

lines.need:
Defense of the Heart resolves
(any opponent has 3+ creatures at your upkeep)
lines.kill: free: search 2 creatures onto battlefield (Craterhoof straight to play → immediate kill)

lines.need:
Razaketh reanimated (Reanimate ~1 mana)
+ creatures to sacrifice
lines.kill: sac fodder, pay 2 life: tutor any card → grab Craterhoof / Finale → kill next turn

lines.need:
Finale of Devastation X≥10 (12 mana, untutorable sorcery)
+ a creature in play
lines.kill: +X/+X & haste to all — but no trample, so it FOCUS-FIRES one opponent

lines.need:
Atraxa swinging (7/7 flying, vigilance, deathtouch, lifelink)
+ a developed board (fallback)
lines.kill: incremental combat — 96% of the deck's decaps; or 21 commander damage
